diagrams.title: Sequential Circuit Block Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the general structure of sequential circuits with combinational logic, storage elements, and feedback path.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates that sequential circuits have memory through feedback of state to input, unlike combinational circuits which have no memory.

diagrams.title: Clock Timing Diagram - Setup and Hold
diagrams.description: Timing diagram showing clock edge, setup time window, hold time window, and valid data region.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates timing constraints for reliable flip-flop operation. Data must be stable during setup+hold window for correct state capture.

diagrams.title: State Diagram: Modulo-3 Counter
diagrams.description: State diagram for synchronous modulo-3 counter with states S0, S1, S2 and transitions on each clock.
diagrams.purpose: Shows graphical representation of sequential circuit behavior. Each state is a circle, transitions are arrows labeled with inputs causing transitions.

diagrams.title: Flip-Flop Comparison Table
diagrams.description: Comparison of SR, JK, D, and T flip-flops with their characteristic equations, truth tables, and typical applications.
elements: SR: Q+ = S + R̅Q, SR=0 constraint
JK: Q+ = JQ̅ + K̅Q, toggle on 11
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for understanding differences between flip-flop types, their characteristic equations, and when to use each.
